name: Vall de Pop Loop from Pedreguer
name_original: Tour por el Río Gorgos – Circular desde Pedreguer pasando por el Meridiano de Greenwich
description: Cycle a moderate 18.2-mile (29.3 km) route from Pedreguer, passing the Gorgos River Viaduct and the Greenwich meridian marker.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Pedreguer offer a diverse landscape for riders, situated at the foot of the Muntanya Gran mountain with views of the Montgó Natural Park and Segaria mountain range. The region features picturesque Orba and Jalon valleys, characterized by extensive olive, citrus, orange, and vineyard groves. Remote canyons and ancient rock formations can be found along various routes, while the Girona River meanders through tranquil paths. This area provides a mix of rural beauty and authentic Spanish charm, away from coastal crowds. Best touring cycling routes around Pedreguer The most popular touring cycling route is Vall de Pop Loop from Pedreguer, an 18.2 miles (29.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 54 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the scenic valleys and agricultural groves. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Santuario de Pla de Petracos – View of Vall d'Ebo loop from la Xara, a difficult 45.4 miles (73.0 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and panoramic mountain vistas. Local touring cyclists also love the Xaló – Carretera de Bérnia loop from la Xara, a 45.2 miles (72.8 km) trail leading through mountain ranges and valleys, often completed in about 4 hours 51 minutes. Touring cycling around Pedreguer is defined by diverse mountain ranges, fertile valleys, and extensive groves. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Pedreguer are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 1600 reviews. More than 37000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Pedreguer's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Pedreguer?
A: Pedreguer offers a diverse landscape for touring cyclists. You'll find routes at the foot of the &lt;strong&gt;Muntanya Gran mountain&lt;/strong&gt;, with views of the &lt;strong&gt;Montgó Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Segaria mountain range&lt;/strong&gt;. The region also features picturesque &lt;strong&gt;Orba and Jalon valleys&lt;/strong&gt;, characterized by extensive olive, citrus, orange, and vineyard groves. Some routes may lead through remote canyons and past ancient rock formations, while others follow tranquil paths along the &lt;strong&gt;Girona River&lt;/strong&gt;.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in the Pedreguer area?
A: There are over 380 touring cycling routes available around Pedreguer, catering to a wide range of preferences and fitness levels. These include easy, moderate, and difficult options, ensuring there's something for every cyclist.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Pedreguer?
A: Yes, Pedreguer offers a good selection of easier routes. Approximately 150 of the touring cycling routes are classified as easy, making them ideal for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. These routes often traverse the scenic valleys and agricultural groves, providing a gentle introduction to the region's beauty.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see while cycling in Pedreguer?
A: The Pedreguer area is rich in natural beauty and interesting landmarks. You might encounter the dramatic landscapes of the &lt;strong&gt;Barranc de l'Infern (Hell's Ravine)&lt;/strong&gt;, enjoy panoramic views from &lt;strong&gt;Monte Pedreguer&lt;/strong&gt;, or cycle through the vibrant &lt;strong&gt;Orba and Jalon valleys&lt;/strong&gt;. For unique geological formations, consider exploring areas near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/68080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova Tallada (Cut Cave)&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/276237" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of the Skulls&lt;/a&gt;. The region also offers stunning coastal views from certain vantage points, despite being inland.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Pedreguer?
A: The touring cycling routes in Pedreguer are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 1600 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse mountain ranges, fertile valleys, and extensive groves, as well as the mix of challenging climbs and serene paths. The region's authentic Spanish charm and quiet roads are also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any routes that offer significant climbs or challenges for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. Pedreguer is known for its challenging climbs, attracting both experienced cyclists and professional training groups. The &lt;strong&gt;Coll de Rates&lt;/strong&gt; is a world-renowned climb, frequently used for its excellent road surface, elegant curves, and breathtaking views. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39541711" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuario de Pla de Petracos – View of Vall d'Ebo loop from la Xara&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39550286" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Xaló – Carretera de Bérnia loop from la Xara&lt;/a&gt; feature significant elevation gains and panoramic mountain vistas, offering a demanding yet rewarding experience.

Q: When is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Pedreguer?
A: Pedreguer is an excellent cycling destination year-round, but spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant conditions with milder temperatures. Winter is also popular, especially for professional teams, due to the region's favorable climate. In February, the almond blossoms create a beautiful landscape, and orange trees provide color throughout the year. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during those months.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Pedreguer?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in Pedreguer are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952691707/vall-de-pop-loop-from-pedreguer" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vall de Pop Loop from Pedreguer&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular route that showcases the scenic valleys and agricultural groves.

Q: Can I find viewpoints along the cycling routes in Pedreguer?
A: Yes, the region is dotted with numerous 'Miradors' or observation points, offering spectacular panoramic views. Driving up &lt;strong&gt;Monte Pedreguer&lt;/strong&gt;, you'll find spots like &lt;strong&gt;Mirabarques&lt;/strong&gt;, providing vistas of Pedreguer, Denia, and Javea. The &lt;strong&gt;Sant Blai chapel&lt;/strong&gt; also offers excellent views of the surrounding mountain ranges. Many routes are designed to pass by these scenic spots, perfect for a break and photo opportunities.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that pass by the coast or offer sea views?
A: While Pedreguer is primarily inland, its proximity to the coast means that some routes offer spectacular views of the Mediterranean Sea. These routes often climb to higher elevations, providing expansive panoramas that stretch towards the coastline, particularly from mountain ranges like Montgó.

Q: Are there options for family-friendly touring cycling in Pedreguer?
A: Yes, Pedreguer offers routes suitable for families, especially among the easier classifications. These routes often follow quieter roads through the picturesque valleys and groves, providing a more relaxed and enjoyable experience for all ages. The well-marked trails and varied difficulty levels mean you can find paths that cater to different family members' abilities.

Q: What kind of support is available for cyclists in Pedreguer?
A: Pedreguer has become a recognized cycling hub, offering various services to support riders. You can find bike rental options and even bike tour guides who possess local knowledge of hidden gems and can customize itineraries to suit your preferences. The town's investment in well-marked routes also enhances the cycling experience.
tips: An obligatory stop when you go out for a ride on the roads of the Marina Alta. A place with everything you need, coffee and bar with a very good offer and quality, a workshop to repair any emergency and a very friendly treatment of all the staff. A highly recommended site!
This geographical point indicates that the Greenwich meridian passes through here. A curious reference to orient yourself in space and time ;)

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: The Tour of the Rio Gorgos – Circular from Pedreguer passing through the Greenwich Meridian is approximately 29.3 kilometers (18.2 miles) long. It has an estimated cycling time of just under two hours, specifically around 1 hour and 54 minutes, for cyclists with good fitness.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: Komoot classifies this as a moderate bike ride. It features an elevation gain of about 360 meters (1,181 feet) over 29.3 km, making it a manageable challenge for those with a reasonable level of cycling fitness. Expect some climbs, but nothing overly strenuous or prolonged.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Tour of the Rio Gorgos?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily characterized by mostly paved surfaces, making it well-suited for road cycling. While generally smooth, some moderate routes on Komoot can include unpaved sections, so be prepared for potential variations, though the majority is accessible.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Tour of the Rio Gorgos?
faqs.answer: The tour is a circular route starting and ending in Pedreguer. While specific parking lots aren't detailed, you can typically find parking within the town of Pedreguer to begin your ride. Look for public parking areas near the town center.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: This route offers a blend of natural and cultural sights. Key highlights include passing by the Gorgos River Viaduct and Town Square, Xaló, and a monument marking the Greenwich meridian. The presence of the Rio Gorgos also provides scenic views along the river valley.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or rest stops on the Tour of the Rio Gorgos?
faqs.answer: Yes, a popular stop along the route is the Velosol Cycling Cafe in Jalón. It's well-known among professional cyclists and enthusiasts for its coffee, bar, and bike workshop, making it an excellent place for a break.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Costa Blanca region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring (March-May) and Autumn (September-November) are often ideal, with milder temperatures and less intense sun than the summer months. Winter can also be good, though cooler.

faqs.question: Is the Tour of the Rio Gorgos cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a cycling route primarily on paved roads, and while dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, it's important to consider their safety and comfort. Cycling with a dog requires specific equipment (e.g., a bike trailer or basket) and ensuring they are accustomed to such activity. Always keep your dog on a leash when off the bike and respect local regulations.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required for this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Tour of the Rio Gorgos – Circular from Pedreguer passing through the Greenwich Meridian. It uses public roads and paths.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the El Camí IT5TR1 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana, Sender de Pedreguer, and El Camí de la Pansa, among others.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: For this moderate cycling tour, we recommend bringing plenty of water, snacks, a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, sun protection (sunscreen, sunglasses), a basic bike repair kit, and a fully charged phone for navigation and emergencies. Don't forget your camera for the scenic views!

waypoints.name: Gorgos River Viaduct and Town Square, Xaló
